dimension_rationales.constraint_strength: FARA requires object/representation, state/record, rule/execution/result, admissibility, resolution, history, provenance, coherence, and target-only recovery separations that GREL does not require of arbitrary packages.
dimension_rationales.cost_relation: GREL has the smaller fixed architecture surface and generic encoder, while FARA provides direct six-axis semantic access and stronger built-in audit constraints. Neither Pareto-dominates under the declared multidimensional cost record.
dimension_rationales.expressiveness: Both pipelines recover the same frozen candidate-neutral finite source projection and produce the same FARA target on the registered image class; GREL also exactly carries finite FARA packages as typed relational data.
dimension_rationales.overall_interpretation: Equivalent bounded representational capacity coexists with stricter FARA admissibility and audit constraints; the declared adapter and constructor reintroduction prevent any primitive-reduction inference.
dimension_rationales.reasoning_specificity: The same factorization succeeds for positive, contrast, and disputed instances. Factorization therefore does not discriminate reasoning.
dimension_rationales.translation: Fixed translations exist in both directions on the declared finite image classes. GREL-to-FARA composes exact recovery with a declared FARA-oriented source adapter and the accepted FARA constructor; FARA-to-GREL uses the single generic encoder.

factorization_contract.candidate_neutrality: admission class, family, title, rationale, and candidate-exposure metadata are excluded from construction
factorization_contract.direct_fara_pipeline: existing tools/s_core_w3_construct.py::construct_witness after the fixed compile_projection adapter
factorization_contract.fara_to_grel_translation: tools/w3_5_grel.py::encode_grel over the finite FARA package
factorization_contract.grel_to_fara_translation: decode_grel, then compile_projection, then existing construct_witness
factorization_contract.identity: construct_witness o compile_projection = (construct_witness o compile_projection o decode_grel) o encode_grel
factorization_contract.source_encoder: tools/w3_5_grel.py::encode_grel over the candidate-neutral authoritative projection
factorization_contract.translation_domain: frozen candidate-neutral finite source projections and finite FARA target packages

nonclaims: reasoning and contrast discrimination is complete
preserved_failures: The GREL-to-FARA translator reuses both a FARA-oriented source adapter and the accepted FARA constructor, so the result is not a primitive reduction.
No reasoning-specific discriminator is established because all three corpus classes factor.
The corpus is finite, synthetic, and not a private holdout.
Full candidate ablation, reconstruction, machinery accounting, and claim-impact closure remain unexecuted.

scope.source_scope: frozen candidate-neutral RCS authoritative projections compiled by one fixed declared FARA-oriented adapter
